item.title: Jonathan Schooler - Consciousness and Time
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4
What is the relationship between consciousness and the nature of ultimate reality? Some say there is no relationship; consciousness is a late, contingent product of biological evolution. Others say consciousness is irreducible and fundamental, whether part of ultimate reality or all of it. Does it make sense to explore consciousness and cosmos together?
Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9
Jonathan Schooler is an American psychologist and Distinguished Professor of Psychological and Brain Sciences at the University of California, Santa Barbara, who researches various topics that intersect aspects of both cognitive psychology and philosophy such as: Belief in free will, Meta-awareness, Mindfulness, Mind-Wandering, Memory, Creativity, and Emotion. Schooler is also known for his sometimes controversial research on topics such as Anomalous Cognition and the decline effect.
Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.

item.title: Teilhard's vision: Cosmic evolution
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded: We’re looking at the Noosphere with fresh eyes. We ask philosopher Clément Vidal: What did Teilhard de Chardin get right or wrong? #shorts

item.title: Dave Plummer: Programming, Autism, and Old-School Microsoft Stories | Lex Fridman Podcast #479
item.author: Lex Fridman
item.content:encoded:
Dave Plummer is a programmer, former Microsoft software engineer (Windows 95, NT, XP), creator of Task Manager, author of two books on autism, and host of the Dave's Garage YouTube channel, where he shares stories from his career, insights on software development, and deep dives into technology. Thank you for listening ❤ Check out our sponsors: https://lexfridman.com/sponsors/ep479-sb See below for timestamps, and to give feedback, submit questions, contact Lex, etc.
*CONTACT LEX:* *Feedback* - give feedback to Lex: https://lexfridman.com/survey *AMA* - submit questions, videos or call-in: https://lexfridman.com/ama *Hiring* - join our team: https://lexfridman.com/hiring *Other* - other ways to get in touch: https://lexfridman.com/contact
*EPISODE LINKS:* Dave's X: https://x.com/davepl1968 Dave's YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@DavesGarage Dave's Secondary YouTube Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@davepl Dave's GitHub: https://github.com/PlummersSoftwareLLC Dave's Books: https://amzn.to/41qd5IB
*SPONSORS:* To support this podcast, check out our sponsors & get discounts: *UPLIFT Desk:* Standing desks and office ergonomics. Go to https://lexfridman.com/s/uplift_desk-ep479-sb *ZocDoc:* App that helps patients find healthcare providers. Go to https://lexfridman.com/s/zocdoc-ep479-sb *Fin:* AI agent for customer service. Go to https://lexfridman.com/s/fin-ep479-sb *Allio Capital:* AI-powered investment app that uses global macroeconomic trends. Go to https://lexfridman.com/s/allio_capital-ep479-sb *Shopify:* Sell stuff online. Go to https://lexfridman.com/s/shopify-ep479-sb
*OUTLINE:* 0:00 - Introduction 1:22 - First computer 7:00 - Dropping out of high-school 14:41 - Joining Microsoft 16:53 - MS-DOS 20:05 - Windows 95 26:52 - The man behind Windows 31:48 - Debugging 37:05 - Task Manager 42:14 - 3D Pinball: Space Cadet 47:13 - Start menu and taskbar 58:12 - Blue Screen of Death 1:00:21 - Best programmers 1:08:22 - Scariest time of Dave's life 1:15:50 - Best Windows version 1:17:40 - Slot machines 1:21:23 - Autism and ADHD 1:40:43 - Fastest programming language 1:44:48 - Future of programming
*PODCAST LINKS:* - Podcast Website: https://lexfridman.com/podcast - Apple Podcasts: https://apple.co/2lwqZIr - Spotify: https://spoti.fi/2nEwCF8 - RSS: https://lexfridman.com/feed/podcast/ - Podcast Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLrAXtmErZgOdP_8GztsuKi9nrraNbKKp4 - Clips Channel: https://www.youtube.com/lexclips

item.title: Mark Bailey on Artificial Intelligence and National Security | Closer To Truth Chats
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Is AI a potential threat to our national security? Dr. Mark Bailey writes about the intersection between artificial intelligence, complexity, and national security. He is an associate professor at the National Intelligence University, where he is the Department Chair for Cyber Intelligence and Data Science, as well as the Director of the Data Science Intelligence Center.
This Chat was made possible by MindFest 2025: "Sentience, Autonomy, and the Future of Human-AI Interaction", a two-day event presented by the Center for the Future Mind.
Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e
To learn more about groundbreaking innovation in AI, neuroscience, and the study of consciousness, visit the Center for the Future Mind, view MindFest videos, and subscribe to their newsletter, visit: https://shorturl.at/mYtqz.

item.title: Oliver Crisp - Jesus as God
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l
Is Jesus God? Obviously, religions differ. But what about within the Christian religion? Is there a unified view of Jesus? Jesus before his human birth? Jesus during his human lifetime? Jesus when dead? Jesus when resurrected? Jesus now? The question is surprisingly complex. One need not be a Christian to appreciate the philosophical conundrums.
Make a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://shorturl.at/Xsn2z
Oliver D. Crisp is a British theologian who currently works as Professor of Analytic Theology at the University of St Andrews, and was formerly a professor of systematic theology at Fuller Theological Seminary in Pasadena, California.
Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.

item.title: Joe Rogan Experience #2372 - Garry Nolan
item.author: PowerfulJRE
item.content:encoded:
Garry Nolan, PhD, is an immunologist and professor at Stanford University School of Medicine. He is also a business executive and Executive Director of the Board of the Sol Foundation, a research and advocacy center focused on UAP studies. https://www.thesolfoundation.org
Hunt with confidence using onX Hunt. Start your free trial today at: https://huntsmarter.smart.link/srwbpznr2
This video is sponsored by BetterHelp. Visit https://BetterHelp.com/JRE

item.title: UFOs, Telepathy, and Alien Encounters: Chris French on the Paranormal | Closer To Truth Chats
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Psychologist Chris French explores why paranormal beliefs—from UFOs and alien encounters to telepathy, ESP, and astrology—continue to persist across cultures and throughout history. Drawing on a psychological perspective, he examines the roots of paranormal experiences, why people believe in the unexplained, and what these phenomena reveal about the human mind. This conversation sheds light on the enduring appeal of the mysterious and challenges us to think critically about the psychology of paranormal belief.
Chris French's book, "The Science of Weird Shit: Why Our Minds Conjure the Paranormal", is available for purchase now: https://amzn.to/43rxMoQ
Chris French is Emeritus Professor and Head of the Anomalistic Psychology Research Unit in the Psychology Department at Goldsmiths, University of London. He is a Fellow of the British Psychological Society and of the Committee for Skeptical Inquiry and a Patron of Humanists UK. He is the coauthor of Anomalistic Psychology: Exploring Paranormal Belief and Experience.
Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

item.title: Rachell Powell - From Hunter-Gatherers to Modern Society
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Closer To Truth is now on Bluesky! Follow us for updates, announcements, and more: https://shorturl.at/GSR4k
Can an evolutionary perspective help explain culture and various social behaviors? Is it proper to use “culture” for non-human animals? If so, what is “animal culture” and how is human culture distinguished?
Make a donation to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/
Rachell Powell is a Professor in the Department of Philosophy at Boston University and Director of the Center for Philosophy and History of Science. She is currently serving as an Associate Editor of "Philosophy & Technology".
Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. item.content:encoded:
Fedor Gorst is a professional pool player whose career highlights include championship wins at the World Nine-Ball Championship, the U.S. Open Pool Championship, and the Derby City Classic. https://www.fedorgorst.com

item.title: Iain McGilchrist - Consciousness and Life After Death?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without paywalls: https://shorturl.at/l3q6G
Life after death is explored in the context of diverse theories of consciousness, from strict Materialism/Physicalism to those of ancient wisdom traditions. We explore the view that the haunting and deeply personal question of life after death relates to theories of consciousness.
Watch more videos on consciousness and life after death here: https://shorturl.at/vNC3W
Iain McGilchrist FRSA is a British psychiatrist, philosopher, and neuroscientist who wrote the 2009 book "The Master and His Emissary: The Divided Brain and the Making of the Western World".
Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

item.title: Philip Clayton - Does God Intervene in Human Affairs?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Support the show with a Closer To Truth merch purchase like a T-shirt, mug, notebook, or hoodie: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje
Is God, if there is a God, the ‘God of History’? Does God make specific interventions in the course of human events to ensure that God’s purpose be fulfilled, for nations as well as for individuals? Or did God so set the laws of nature before the foundation of the world such that God’s goals would be achieved without God’s special, active interventions?
Make a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://closertotruth.com/donate/
Philip Clayton is an American philosopher of religion and philosopher of science. His work focuses on the intersection of science, ethics, and society. He currently holds the Ingraham Chair at Claremont School of Theology and serves as an affiliated faculty member at Claremont Graduate University.
Closer To Truth is now on Bluesky! Follow us for updates, announcements, and more: https://shorturl.at/Zbk40
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.

item.title: Donald Hoffman on spacetime
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded: When we talk about understanding the universe, do we have the right framework? Maybe we need to think about it in a completely new way. Physicist Donald Hoffman argues that we should see spacetime as a headset to the universe. #shorts

item.title: Can We Explore Transcendence Via Art and Mind? | Episode 2501 | Closer To Truth
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
What is the three-way relationship among mind, art, and transcendence? What is it about art that can elicit the sense of transcendence, going beyond the self, bursting the constraining bonds of semantic language? What is it about transcendence that it can be expressed by art? What in fact is transcendence, from a momentary escape from self to a full- blown mystical experience? How does the art-transcendence nexus engage the mind? We describe key terms and ideas.
Featuring interviews with Simone Schnall, Julio Bermudez, Judith Wolfe, Oludamini Ogunnaike, Jonathan Berger, and Anjan Chatterjee.
Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.

item.title: Robin Hanson on AI and Large Language Models | Closer To Truth Chats
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Robots may one day rule the world, but what is a robot-ruled Earth like? Dr. Robin Hanson explores the implications of a future world in which researchers have not created artificial general intelligence but have learned to copy humans onto computers, creating "ems," or emulated people, who quickly come to outnumber the real ones.
Robin Hanson is an Associate Professor of Economics, and received his Ph.D in social sciences from Caltech. He joined George Mason's economics faculty in 1999 after completing a two-year post-doc at U.C Berkely. His major fields of interest include health policy, regulation, and formal political theory.
Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e
This Chat was made possible by MindFest 2025: "Sentience, Autonomy, and the Future of Human-AI Interaction", a two-day event presented by the Center for the Future Mind.
To learn more about groundbreaking innovation in AI, neuroscience, and the study of consciousness, visit the Center for the Future Mind, view MindFest videos, and subscribe to their newsletter, visit: https://shorturl.at/mYtqz.

item.title: Scott Horton: The Case Against War and the Military Industrial Complex | Lex Fridman Podcast #478
item.author: Lex Fridman
item.content:encoded:
Scott Horton is the director of the Libertarian Institute, editorial director of Antiwar.com, host of The Scott Horton Show, co-host of Provoked, and for the past three decades a staunch critic of U.S. military interventionism. Thank you for listening ❤ Check out our sponsors: https://lexfridman.com/sponsors/ep478-sb See below for timestamps, and to give feedback, submit questions, contact Lex, etc.
*CONTACT LEX:* *Feedback* - give feedback to Lex: https://lexfridman.com/survey *AMA* - submit questions, videos or call-in: https://lexfridman.com/ama *Hiring* - join our team: https://lexfridman.com/hiring *Other* - other ways to get in touch: https://lexfridman.com/contact

item.title: Galen Strawson - Are There Too Many Theories of Consciousness?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Follow us on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/
What is the current state of the consciousness studies field? What are the major issues and trends? What kinds of experiments are being conducted? What kinds of arguments are being made?
Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq
Galen John Strawson is a British analytic philosopher and literary critic who works primarily on philosophy of mind, metaphysics, John Locke, David Hume, Immanuel Kant, and Friedrich Nietzsche.
Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. item.title: Alan Torrance - How Does Salvation Work?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4
The Christian claim is that salvation is the ultimate goal of human life. But what literally is salvation and how does one obtain it? How to comprehend each element of the salvation process and the way they work together? One need not be a Christian to appreciate the philosophical conundrums.
Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9
Alan Torrance is a Scottish theologian, academic and ordained minister of the Church of Scotland. Torrance was ordained as a minister in the Church of Scotland in 1984. His academic career began at the Knox Theological Hall and the University of Otago, Dunedin, New Zealand.
Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

item.author: PowerfulJRE
item.content:encoded:

item.title: Liad Mudrik - Theories of Consciousness
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Closer To Truth is now on Bluesky! Follow us for updates, announcements, and more: https://shorturl.at/GSR4k
Explore diverse theories of consciousness including materialism, dualism, panpsychism, idealism and diverse ways of thinking about consciousness from the perspectives of neuroscience, philosophy, wisdom traditions. Compare and contrast diverse theories of consciousness as they are arrayed on a “Landscape of Consciousness”.
Make a donation to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/
Professor Liad Mudrik is a researcher at the school of psychological sciences and Sagol school of neuroscience at Tel Aviv University. Her research focuses on conscious experience, its neural mechanisms and functions.
Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. item.title: Magic, wonder, and power?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded: Philosopher Helen De Cruz explains why ancient philosophers were interested in magic #shorts

item.title: Stephen Wolfram on Foundations, Computation, and Cellular Autometa | Closer To Truth Chats
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
How does complexity emerge from simplicity? In this interview, Stephen Wolfram explores the concept of universal computation and how simple rules can generate intricate, seemingly random patterns. This perspective challenges traditional views of complexity in physics, biology, and beyond, revealing deep insights into the computational universe.
From the foundations of cellular automata to the discovery of surprising patterns in nature, Wolfram shows how computation reshapes our understanding of reality and revolutionizes the way we think about science.
Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e
Stephen Wolfram is a British-American computer scientist, physicist, and businessman. He is known for his work in computer algebra and theoretical physics. In 2012, he was named a fellow of the American Mathematical Society.
